Title: Conserved but Attenuated Parental Gene Expression in Allopolyploids: Constitutive Zinc Hyperaccumulation in the Allotetraploid Arabidopsis kamchatica

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High Zn contributes to heavy metal tolerance/hyperaccumulation and may support elemental defense; PDF1.1 itself is also linked to fungal resistance and heavy metal tolerance
Common_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: A. halleri subsp. gemmifera (Tada mine); A. lyrata subsp. petraea w1178
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Extremely high in A. halleri versus A. lyrata before Zn treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Among top four genes with >5-fold H-origin bias in leaves; H-origin homeolog downregulated after Zn treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to Zn tolerance/hyperaccumulation-associated detoxification capacity in A. kamchatica through strongly biased H-origin expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq homeolog expression; prior yeast and Arabidopsis transformation evidence cited
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDF1.1 showed one of the strongest H-origin expression biases in leaves. 2. H-origin expression was downregulated after Zn treatment. 3. Prior studies cited support a direct role in Zn tolerance, linking this biased expression to A. kamchatica hyperaccumulation physiology.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May couple metal tolerance with defense signaling changes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because it is neither enzyme/pathway catalyst nor transcriptional regulator
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tolerance-stacking gene with HMA4/MTP1 for heavy-metal engineering

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Among strongest A. halleri-biased genes in leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 3–5-fold H-origin bias in at least one sample; H-origin copy downregulated after Zn treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate transporter contributing to conserved A. halleri-derived Zn homeostasis program in A. kamchatica
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IRT3 showed H-origin-biased expression in A. kamchatica. 2. H-origin IRT3 was downregulated after Zn treatment, fitting the constitutive hyperaccumulator response model.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned among hyperaccumulation-related candidates profiled in Results
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible auxiliary target for Zn homeostasis engineering

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 3–5-fold H-origin bias in at least one tissue; higher H-origin expression in roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate metal-homeostasis gene associated with inherited H-origin expression bias in A. kamchatica
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IAR1 showed significant H-origin-biased expression, especially in roots. 2. Expression pattern is consistent with retention of A. halleri metal-homeostasis network.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional contribution not directly tested in this paper

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate contributor to root metal homeostasis in A. kamchatica
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HMA5 was among root heavy-metal genes with significant H-origin-biased expression. 2. Supports conservation of A. halleri-derived metal-transport program.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not functionally characterized in this paper beyond expression bias

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Among root genes with significant homeolog ratio changes after Zn treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate Zn-homeostasis transporter responsive to Zn stress in roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZIP10 was identified among significant root ratio-change genes after Zn treatment. 2. It is a candidate transporter contributing to stress-responsive Zn homeostasis.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Among root genes with significant homeolog ratio changes after Zn treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate Zn tolerance/homeostasis factor in roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HIPP25 was among significant root ratio-change genes under Zn treatment. 2. Annotation suggests participation in metal tolerance/homeostasis.

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Among root genes with significant homeolog ratio changes after Zn treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate heavy-metal transport component responsive to Zn treatment in roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. This unnamed heavy metal transport protein was among significant root ratio-change genes. 2. It is a candidate component of Zn homeostasis in A. kamchatica.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene symbol not provided in paper

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: AT2G19110; A. halleri contig C1829917.g40478; A. lyrata scaffold16994.g19792
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Root xylem loading and long-distance Zn/Cd transport
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ATP-dependent efflux/transport of Zn2+ (and Cd2+) across membranes to enable xylem loading and root-to-shoot translocation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream transport/delivery step controlling shoot accumulation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly enables transport and accumulation of Zn in shoots/leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential micronutrient; at high leaf levels contributes to metal tolerance/hyperaccumulation and elemental defense against herbivory
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: A. halleri subsp. gemmifera (Tada mine); A. lyrata subsp. petraea w1178
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MUR; PAK; also ALK, SRM, HMK in some assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in A. halleri than A. lyrata; higher in roots than leaves; constitutively high before Zn treatment and downregulated after Zn stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong H-origin > L-origin bias in leaves and roots; high before Zn treatment, then downregulated after 48 h 500 µM Zn in shoots and roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydroponic Zn treatment, especially 500 µM Zn for 48 h (transcriptomics) and 7 d (ionomics); additional 250–1000 µM Zn in phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Zn root-to-shoot transport and leaf hyperaccumulation in A. kamchatica; H-origin expression contributes to retained but attenuated Zn hyperaccumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq homeolog expression analysis; pyrosequencing validation; genomic pyrosequencing copy-number assay; prior functional evidence cited from RNAi in planta
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for Zn root-to-shoot transport and major determinant of hyperaccumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. H-origin HMA4 showed among the strongest expression biases over L-origin homeologs in leaves and roots. 2. HMA4 was highly expressed before Zn treatment and downregulated after Zn stress in A. halleri and A. kamchatica. 3. Genomic pyrosequencing indicated a 3:1 H-origin:L-origin ratio, consistent with three A. halleri-derived HMA4 copies in A. kamchatica. 4. The H-origin HMA4 region showed strongly reduced diversity, consistent with inheritance of the A. halleri sweep/hyperaccumulation haplotype.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (DDBJ DRA004363, DRA003243; BioProject PRJDB4054; BioSample SAMD00035297; Experiment DRX049283; Run DRR054434); resequencing of 20 A. kamchatica genotypes; ionomics
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Attenuated total expression in allotetraploid relative to A. halleri likely reduces Zn hyperaccumulation to about half; possible interaction with A. lyrata-derived network may reduce transport efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a biosynthetic enzyme but a transporter central to nutrient accumulation; paper demonstrates inherited triplication from A. halleri founder and selective-sweep-like low diversity around H-origin locus
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering Zn hyperaccumulation/biofortification or phytoremediation, but likely needs copy-number/promoter strength tuning and detoxification-gene stacking to avoid toxicity

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates transport/sequestration of Zn2+ and Cd2+ into vacuolar compartment for detoxification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Detoxification/storage step parallel to long-distance transport
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product by vacuolar sequestration of Zn
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Micronutrient and hyperaccumulated metal contributing to tolerance/elemental defense
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: A. halleri subsp. gemmifera (Tada mine); A. lyrata subsp. petraea w1178
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Much higher in A. halleri than A. lyrata in prior comparisons cited
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: One of the top genes with >5-fold H-origin expression bias in leaves; also higher H-origin expression in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to Zn detoxification/sequestration supporting hyperaccumulation in A. kamchatica through preferential H-origin expression
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq homeolog expression; pyrosequencing validation; prior cited complementation and localization evidence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HMA3 showed one of the strongest H-origin over L-origin expression biases. 2. Its expression pattern matches known hyperaccumulator-associated genes. 3. Preferential H-origin expression supports inherited Zn detoxification capacity in the allotetraploid.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced sequestration could alter Zn partitioning among tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function discussed mainly via prior studies; included here because it is a principal experimentally profiled candidate in Results
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking target with HMA4/MTP1 for Zn tolerance and controlled accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transports Zn2+ from cytoplasm into intracellular compartments for detoxification
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Detoxification/storage step downstream of Zn uptake/translocation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product by cellular detoxification of excess Zn
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Micronutrient; excess Zn detoxification enables hyperaccumulation
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: A. halleri subsp. gemmifera (Tada mine); A. lyrata subsp. petraea w1178
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in A. halleri than A. lyrata; dramatically elevated in A. halleri in cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Top four genes with >5-fold H-origin expression bias in leaves; higher H-origin expression in roots; H-origin homeolog upregulated ~1.5-fold after Zn treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Zn detoxification/tolerance and supports leaf hyperaccumulation in A. kamchatica; induced after Zn exposure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq homeolog expression; pyrosequencing validation; prior QTL and functional evidence cited
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control locus for Zn tolerance, but not proven sole rate-limiting step in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MTP1 displayed very strong H-origin expression bias, especially in leaves. 2. H-origin MTP1 increased after Zn treatment, unlike several transporters that decreased. 3. Results support a detoxification role needed after Zn reaches shoots. 4. Prior evidence cited identifies MTP1 as a large-effect Zn-tolerance locus.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stronger sequestration may alter partitioning and potentially reduce translocation if expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A. halleri duplicates likely collapsed in assembly; observed expression may represent summed duplicated H-origin copies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent companion target to HMA4 for improving Zn tolerance without lethal cytosolic accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transports divalent metal ions including Zn-associated substrates across intracellular membranes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports precursor/ion homeostasis associated with Zn accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: A. halleri subsp. gemmifera; A. lyrata subsp. petraea
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in A. halleri than A. lyrata
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 3–5-fold H-origin bias in at least one sample; strong H-origin bias validated by pyrosequencing; downregulated after Zn treatment in H-origin homeologs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with conserved A. halleri-derived Zn homeostasis program in A. kamchatica
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NRAMP3 showed significant H-origin-biased expression. 2. Pyrosequencing confirmed H-origin bias. 3. H-origin homeolog expression decreased after Zn treatment, consistent with constitutive hyperaccumulator response.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific transport substrate not fully resolved in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible supporting target in multigene Zn homeostasis engineering

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates Zn2+ transport across membranes as part of Zn homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly influences Zn distribution contributing to leaf accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: A. halleri subsp. gemmifera; A. lyrata subsp. petraea
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in A. halleri than A. lyrata
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher H-origin expression in roots; downregulated in A. halleri and H-origin homeologs after Zn treatment; significant homeolog ratio changes in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate contributor to Zn homeostasis and treatment-responsive homeolog ratio change in A. kamchatica
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression and ratio-change analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZIP3 showed H-origin-biased constitutive expression. 2. H-origin ZIP3 was downregulated following Zn treatment. 3. ZIP3 was one of few metal-homeostasis genes with significant homeolog ratio change in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homeolog ratio plasticity suggests mixed cis/trans regulation under Zn stress
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential fine-tuning target for Zn partitioning

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates Zn2+ transport across membranes in Zn homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly influences Zn distribution contributing to accumulation phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: A. halleri subsp. gemmifera; A. lyrata subsp. petraea
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in A. halleri than A. lyrata
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher H-origin expression in roots; downregulated after Zn treatment in A. halleri and H-origin homeologs; significant homeolog ratio change in roots validated by pyrosequencing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate contributor to Zn homeostasis with stress-responsive homeolog modulation in roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZIP9 had higher H-origin expression in roots. 2. It was downregulated after Zn treatment in A. halleri and H-origin homeologs. 3. A significant control-vs-Zn homeolog ratio change in roots was validated by pyrosequencing.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Stress-responsive balance between homeologs may modulate root Zn retention vs transport
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning root/shoot Zn partitioning

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely transports Zn2+ into vacuolar compartment to reduce cytosolic Zn
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch by increasing root retention and potentially reducing shoot Zn transport
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significant homeolog ratio change in roots; strong upregulation of L-origin copy after Zn treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: May promote root Zn retention and contribute to lower shoot transport in A. kamchatica relative to A. halleri
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MTP3 was among significant root ratio-change genes. 2. Strong induction of the L-origin copy after Zn treatment shifted the homeolog ratio. 3. Authors propose this could contribute to longer root retention and lower Zn transport to leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce desirable shoot Zn accumulation by sequestering Zn in roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted in Discussion as a candidate inhibitory influence from the A. lyrata-derived network
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential editing target to reduce root sequestration if greater shoot Zn accumulation is desired

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes nicotianamine, a metal chelator involved in metal transport/homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Precursor/supporting metabolite supply for metal transport
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies chelator pool supporting Zn transport/homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Among significant ratio-change genes in roots after Zn treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate contributor to Zn homeostasis through nicotianamine-mediated metal handling in roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAS4 was among root genes with significant homeolog ratio changes under Zn treatment. 2. Its known function suggests altered chelator supply may participate in Zn homeostasis in A. kamchatica.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence in this paper is associative via ratio-change analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible support target for metal homeostasis engineering

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Activated methyl cycle / nicotianamine precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts methionine and ATP to S-adenosyl-L-methionine, precursor for nicotianamine synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for metal-chelator biosynthesis affecting Zn homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Micronutrient; transport/homeostasis can depend on chelator metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Arabidopsis halleri-derived homeologs and Arabidopsis lyrata-derived homeologs analyzed separately
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Among root genes showing significant homeolog ratio changes after Zn treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate supporting gene for Zn homeostasis via precursor supply to nicotianamine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAM2 displayed significant treatment-dependent homeolog ratio changes in roots. 2. Its biochemical role links methyl-cycle flux to metal-chelator synthesis relevant to Zn handling.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad metabolic role may cause pleiotropy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Association only; not directly functionally validated in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Secondary target; caution due to central metabolism role
